sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
To ensure her kidneys are looking good right now and to schedule a kidney ultrasound.
History of Present Illness
The patient's twin, Katherine, was born prematurely at 34 and 3 weeks and weighed 3 pounds 8 ounces and 3 pounds 13 ounces. She experienced feeding difficulties, including pauses in breathing during feeding, and was subsequently put on a cow's milk-free diet. She has been diagnosed with Smith-Magenis syndrome, which is associated with congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract, and has experienced reflex issues. She has received extensive therapy, including feeding, occupational, and physical therapy, and has been seen by an allergist, neurologist, and ophthalmologist. The patient reports that she has had many therapies and that her family has seen many specialists. She is currently taking ripple pea protein milk and water. She is now crawling and trying to take steps. She has a lot of wet diapers, around 8 to 10 per day, and drinks a lot. She is 22 months old and is approximately the same size as her twin, who is a girl.
Findings
Physical exam reveals a well-nourished female who is slightly fussy when examined. Her eyes appear small, and she exhibits mild hypotonia of the lower extremities. External genitalia are normal. The doctor listened to her heart and lungs and examined her ears.
Assessment
medical_concepts: congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Katherine is a 22-month-old former 34 and 3-week-old, twin with Smith-Magenis syndrome. She has congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract, and may require further evaluation.
Plan
We will obtain the screening of the kidneys by ultrasound today. If there are abnormalities on the kidney ultrasound, we will determine next steps and future follow-up. The family lives in Dallas, Georgia, so her follow-up should be at the town center location. I will call you with the results. If it's normal, you won't need to see me again, but if it's abnormal, you can see me in Kennesaw. The nurse will be in to have you complete some paperwork and give you instructions for the ultrasound.
medical_concepts: congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract
congenital anomalies of the kidney and urinary tract
